variants: what is on the KNM exam
keyword.searcher_intent: Understand what changed in the KNM exam after July 2025, what topics to study, how to prepare for the new format

audience.pain_summary: Confused about KNM exam changes in July 2025. Worried outdated study materials won't prepare them. Need clarity on new topics and format.

angle: The KNM exam changed significantly in July 2025. Most study materials are outdated. This guide covers exactly what changed, the new topics (digital literacy, Holocaust, women's rights, healthcare), and how to prepare.
thesis_sentence: The KNM exam that took effect July 2025 shifted from testing 'what you should do' to 'what is true about Dutch society' — here's exactly what changed and how to prepare for the new format.

outline.section: The KNM Exam Changed in July 2025 — Here's What You Need to Know
outline.notes: Hook with the key shift: old KNM tested desired behavior ('what should you do?'), new KNM tests factual knowledge ('what is true?'). Many people studying with old materials will be surprised.
Quick explainer for people who don't know. KNM = Kennis van de Nederlandse Maatschappij. 40 video-based multiple choice questions. 70% to pass (28/40). Part of inburgering alongside language exams.

outline.section: The Big Shift: From 'What Should You Do?' to 'What Is True?'
outline.notes: Core change explained. Old exam: 'Your neighbor has a birthday. What do you do?' New exam: 'In the Netherlands, it is common to congratulate someone on their birthday.' Focus on understanding society, not performing expected behavior.
outline.evidence_needed: IamExpat and DutchReview coverage of changes

outline.section: New Topic 1: Digital Literacy and DigiD
outline.notes: New in 2025. Questions about DigiD, protecting personal information online, navigating official Dutch websites. Why this matters for daily life in NL.

outline.section: New Topic 2: The Holocaust and WWII in the Netherlands
outline.notes: New historical focus. Questions about what happened during WWII, impact on Dutch Jewish communities. Why the exam now includes this.

outline.section: New Topic 3: Women's Rights and Autonomy
outline.notes: Explicit focus on women's freedom to make life choices, work, live independently. The exam tests understanding that women have full equality in NL.

outline.section: New Topic 4: Healthcare System Deep Dive
outline.notes: More detailed healthcare questions than before. Role of praktijkondersteuner (GP support staff), how to navigate the Dutch healthcare system, referral process.

outline.section: Topics That Remain: What's Still on the Exam
outline.notes: Quick overview of continuing topics: housing, education, politics/government, history and geography. The exam is still 40 questions covering broad Dutch society knowledge.

outline.section: Common KNM Topics and Sample Questions
outline.notes: Practical examples of question types. Healthcare, education system, housing rules, political structure. Give people a sense of what to expect.

outline.section: How to Prepare: Updated Study Resources
outline.notes: Where to find updated materials. DUO practice exams (now updated), which course providers have updated their KNM content. Warn against outdated 2024 materials.

outline.section: KNM vs Language Exams: Different Preparation
outline.notes: KNM is factual memorization, language exams are skill-based. You can cram KNM in 1-2 weeks if language is solid. Don't neglect it but don't over-prepare either.
Practical logistics. Video-based questions, multiple choice, 45 minutes, scoring system. Book via DUO, €50 fee.

value_props_to_weave.pain: Worried about preparing with outdated materials
value_props_to_weave.prop: Clear explanation of exactly what changed and what to study
value_props_to_weave.proof: Specific new topics from official sources

value_props_to_weave.pain: KNM feels like another overwhelming hurdle
value_props_to_weave.prop: KNM is actually the easiest part of inburgering if you prepare correctly
value_props_to_weave.proof: Factual memorization vs skill-building — different preparation approach

originality_nuggets: Most KNM guides are outdated (pre-July 2025). This covers the actual current exam.
Specific breakdown of each new topic area with examples
Practical study timeline advice: KNM can be crammed, language cannot

seo.title_tag: KNM Exam 2025-2026: What Changed and How to Prepare
seo.meta_description: The KNM exam changed in July 2025 with new topics: digital literacy, Holocaust, women's rights, healthcare. Complete guide to the new format and how to pass.

aeo_directives.featured_snippet_target: What changed in the KNM exam 2025?
aeo_directives.definition_paragraph: The KNM (Knowledge of Dutch Society) exam changed significantly on July 1, 2025. The main shift is from testing 'desired behavior' to testing 'factual knowledge' — instead of asking what you should do in situations, the exam now asks what is true about Dutch society. New topics include digital literacy (DigiD, online safety), the Holocaust and WWII in the Netherlands, women's rights and autonomy, and detailed healthcare system knowledge. The format remains 40 video-based multiple choice questions with a 70% passing threshold (28/40).
